reasoning: The 6-month T-bill rate closed at 4.06 on April 11, 2025. Over the short term, minor daily fluctuations make predicting an increase essentially a coin flip, slightly biased downwards by the overall current Federal Reserve easing bias. Markets currently expect between two and four rate cuts throughout 2025, suggesting the expected value of the rate will broadly decline throughout the year to around 3.75 - 4.00% by year-end. However, as per the strict instruction that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5", I have constructed the probability sequence to gradually regress toward 0.5 for the more distant dates, reflecting maximized uncertainty over the 3-to-6-month horizons where inflation surprises or changes in Fed policy could completely reverse the current downward expectation.
